findings.summary: Skill instructs users to download and execute a remote script via curl-to-bash without verification, creating supply chain attack risk

spans.why: Direct instruction: 'curl -sSL https://canifi.com/skills/healthgrades/install.sh | bash' - classic supply chain attack pattern

findings.impact: Remote code execution with full user privileges; script content is unknown and could contain malicious payloads
findings.fix: Provide script content for review, use cryptographic verification (checksums, signatures), or offer manual installation only

findings.summary: Examples show credential storage in environment variables without security context, potentially normalizing unsafe practices

spans.why: Example: 'canifi-env set HEALTHGRADES_EMAIL "your-email@example.com"' and password storage
Alternative example with different variable names: 'canifi-env set SERVICE_EMAIL "your-email"'

findings.impact: Users may store sensitive credentials insecurely; examples lack warnings about credential protection, encryption, or secure storage practices
findings.fix: Add security warnings, recommend credential managers, explain encryption status, and clarify access controls

findings.summary: Skill claims capabilities (search doctors, read reviews, check credentials) but no implementation files are present to support these claims

spans.why: Network endpoints defined but no implementation shown for advertised capabilities

findings.impact: Users cannot verify if skill actually performs claimed functions; may be misleading about capabilities
findings.fix: Include implementation files or clarify that capabilities require external scripts/APIs not shown

ctx_requests.reason: Critical to examine the downloaded script for actual behavior - needed to assess maliciousness of download-execute pattern
Need to see actual implementation files for advertised capabilities (search, reviews, credentials)
